Papaver rhoeas is a flowering plant belonging to the family Papaveraceae, characterized by its bright red or pink flowers and large, seed-filled capsules.

The seeds of Papaver rhoeas have been used in traditional medicine for their potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, as well as their ability to support digestive health and reduce stress.

The bioactive constituents of Papaver rhoeas seeds include alkaloids, such as protopine and sanguinarine, as well as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins.

The most common herbal preparations made with Papaver rhoeas seeds are teas, capsules, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and reduce stress.
